text: A twenty-two year old unmarried lady presented with severe pain in the abdomen was diagnosed to have a twisted parovarian cyst on the left side.

The surgeon begins the parovarian cystectomy by cutting the anti-mycentric border of the broad ligament. A broad incision is made, and gentle traction and counter traction are applied to separate the cyst from its bed within the mesentery of the broad ligament, avoiding damage to fragile blood vessels. At the final point of attachment, coagulation is applied, allowing for the complete removal of the cyst.
The surgeon ruptures the cyst, allowing the fluid to escape. The cyst is then ribbonized within the peritoneal cavity before being removed and sent for histopathological examination.
